food_subgroup: Prepared chicken meal bowl with grains and vegetables

ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn term exists for a composed energy bowl with rotisserie chicken and black pepper nuggets. FOODON_00002473 (chicken dish) is the closest defensible parent category.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP (chicken meat, cooked) is used as the closest parent. No composite bowl code exists in FoodEx2 for this specific meal format.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: SNOMED CT 226529007 (chicken dish) is the closest available parent concept. No specific code for energy bowl with rotisserie chicken and black pepper nuggets exists.

main_ingredients: mixed grains (brown rice or quinoa base)

serving.natural_language_portion: One energy bowl meal as served at Subway Singapore, approximately 450 g, comprising rotisserie chicken, black pepper nuggets, a grain base, and salad vegetables.

nutrient_source.title: USDA FoodData Central — Chicken, rotisserie, breast meat, cooked; combined with USDA SR Legacy — Chicken nuggets, breaded, cooked; grain and vegetable components estimated from USDA Foundation Foods
nutrient_source.organization: United States Department of Agriculture (USDA) Agricultural Research Service
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: No direct nutrient entry exists for this specific Subway Singapore energy bowl product. Values are estimated by component-weighted averaging: rotisserie chicken breast (~40% by weight, USDA FDC #331960), black pepper breaded chicken nuggets (~20% by weight, USDA SR Legacy #21069), mixed grain base — brown rice and quinoa (~25% by weight, USDA FDC #168878 and #168917), and mixed salad vegetables with dressing (~15% by weight, USDA FDC #169967). Per-100g values are derived from this weighted composite. Confidence is medium due to estimation.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson FS et al.; brown rice GI ~50, chicken and protein components GI ~0, composite bowl estimated GI ~48
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific Subway energy bowl. GI is estimated at 48 (low) based on the dominant carbohydrate source being brown rice / mixed grains (GI ~50 per University of Sydney GI Database, Atkinson et al. 2021) and quinoa (GI ~53), weighted against the high protein and fat content of the chicken components which blunt postprandial glycemic response. Composite meal GI estimated at 48. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (48 × 56.25) / 100 ≈ 27.0, rounded to 26.4 using 55 g available carbohydrate per 450 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content approximately 380 mg per 100 g; a full 450 g serving delivers approximately 1710 mg sodium, representing 74–85% of the WHO recommended daily limit of 2000 mg.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should consume this meal with caution due to its high sodium load from seasoned chicken, black pepper nugget breading, and dressing; requesting reduced-sodium dressing or sauce on the side is advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic index (~48) and moderate glycemic load (~26) per serving; high protein content (13.2 g per 100 g) helps attenuate postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes can include this bowl in a structured meal plan given its low-to-moderate GI and high protein content, but should monitor portion size and avoid high-sugar dressings to keep the overall glycemic load within target.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat 1.4 g per 100 g (6.3 g per serving) and sodium 380 mg per 100 g (1710 mg per serving); breaded nugget component contributes trans fat and saturated fat.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should be mindful of the sodium and saturated fat contributed by the black pepper nuggets and dressing; choosing grilled chicken only and requesting dressing on the side would reduce cardiovascular risk.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The Energy Bowl with Rotisserie Chicken and Black Pepper Nuggets is a composed Western-style grain bowl meal sold at Subway Singapore and related franchise outlets. It combines slow-roasted rotisserie chicken — tender, mildly seasoned, and high in lean protein — with crispy black pepper-seasoned breaded chicken nuggets, served over a base of mixed grains such as brown rice or quinoa, and accompanied by fresh salad greens and vegetables. The bowl is finished with a dressing. The rotisserie chicken contributes a smoky, savoury flavour, while the black pepper nuggets add a spiced, crunchy contrast. The grain base provides complex carbohydrates and dietary fibre, making this a moderately filling meal. Nutritionally, the bowl is a high-protein, moderate-carbohydrate meal with approximately 148 kcal per 100 g and 13.2 g protein per 100 g. The sodium content is elevated due to seasoning and dressing, making it a concern for individuals managing hypertension. The glycemic index is estimated at 48 (low), driven by the brown rice and quinoa base, with the high protein content further blunting postprandial glucose response. This meal is suitable for individuals seeking a protein-rich, grain-based lunch or dinner option in a fast-casual setting. It is not suitable for vegetarians or vegans. Halal status is generally maintained at Subway Singapore outlets. Clinical consideration should be given to sodium intake for hypertensive patients and portion control for those managing Type 2 Diabetes or cardiovascular disease.

confidence_reason: No direct nutrient entry exists for this specific Subway Singapore energy bowl product in any approved primary source (HPB Singapore Foodo, USDA FoodData Central, or ICMR-NIN). Nutrient values are estimated by component-weighted averaging of USDA FDC entries for rotisserie chicken breast, breaded chicken nuggets, brown rice, quinoa, and mixed vegetables. GI is analogously estimated from the University of Sydney GI Database using the grain base as the primary glycemic driver. Confidence is therefore medium.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are component-weighted estimates derived from USDA FoodData Central and USDA SR Legacy entries; no single approved-source entry exists for this exact Subway Singapore product. 2. Serving size of 450 g is an estimate based on typical Subway bowl portion sizes; actual weight may vary by outlet and customisation. 3. Vitamin D is -1 as it is not reported in the USDA component sources used for this estimation. 4. Vitamin E is -1 as it is not reported consistently across the component sources used for this estimation. 5. GI of 48 is an analogous estimate; no direct GI measurement for this specific product was found in the University of Sydney GI Database or peer-reviewed literature. 6. Halal status reflects general Subway Singapore policy; individual outlet certification should be verified. 7. Trans fat content (0.1 g per 100 g) is attributed to the breaded nugget component and is within typical ranges for commercially breaded chicken products per USDA SR Legacy. 8. Sodium is elevated and represents a primary clinical concern for hypertensive individuals consuming a full serving. 9. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are estimated from USDA component sources and carry medium confidence.
